title: Automation Changes
excerpt: I would suggest that as automations are sometimes temperamental, especially when moving items to the board they are on.  It would be a good idea to be able to switch all automations on and off at the same time. I have ma…

posts.author: Jacob123 (Jacob)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would suggest that as automations are sometimes temperamental, especially when moving items to the board they are on. It would be a good idea to be able to switch all automations on and off at the same time. I have made hundreds of automations for my company, and find it hard to manage them all. I also believe it would be a good idea to be able to group automation based on what tasks they are affiliated with, and can also switch them on and off in these groups.

I am aware these options are not currently available, however, I believe this would really important for the biggest automation users. when you have over 100 automations on a board, it is currently very difficult to manage.

posts.author: alicerichard65 (Alice Richard)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would suggest that as automations are sometimes temperamental, especially when moving items to the board they are on. It would be a good idea to be able to switch all automations on and off at the same time. I have made hundreds of automations for my company, and find it hard to manage them all. I also believe it would be a good idea to be able to group automation based on what tasks they are affiliated with, and can also switch them on and off in these groups Official Website

I am aware these options are not currently available, however, I believe this would really important for the biggest automation users. when you have over 100 automations on a board, it is currently very difficult to manage.

I can see how managing a large number of automations can become quite challenging. It makes sense to have options for toggling all automations on and off simultaneously and grouping them by tasks for better management. While these features might not be available at the moment, sharing your feedback with the platform’s support or feature request forums could be a great step.

In the meantime, organizing your automations by naming conventions or using tags (if available) might help make them more manageable
